avalancheActivity.highlights: The conditions are dangerous for backcountry touring and other off-piste activities. Careful route selection is required.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of the strong northerly foehn wind, fresh snow drift accumulations will form. High-altitude starting zones will be replenished as a consequence of the wind. Here medium-sized and large natural dry avalanches are possible.
In the typical avalanche paths the avalanches can as before reach fairly large size.
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation moist and wet avalanches are possible as the day progresses, even large ones in isolated cases.
Single winter sport participants can release avalanches easily, including large ones. Caution is to be exercised on steep slopes, and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Little snow fell during the night. Over a wide area 10 to 30 cm of snow has fallen above approximately 1800 m.
Over a wide area 60 to 100 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Sunday above approximately 1800 m.
Until Saturday the wind will be strong over a wide area.
The snowpack is unfavourably layered.
Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Some large and, in isolated cases, very large powder avalanches have been released on Tuesday. Isolated medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large slab avalanches have been released by people on Thursday.

tendency.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already increase in the late morning.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of new snow and wind a dangerous avalanche situation will persist in some regions.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of snowfall and the occasionally storm force northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days. This applies in particular in the regions exposed to the foehn wind above the tree line, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls.
The avalanche-prone wind slabs can be released easily, even by a single winter sport participant,.
Along the border with France the avalanche prone locations are more widespread and the danger is level 4 (high).

On wind-loaded slopes and in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place individual large and, in isolated cases, very large powder avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some regions over a wide area 60 to 100 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Monday. Over a wide area 15 to 20 cm of snow fell yesterday in all altitude zones. The sometimes storm force wind has transported a lot of snow.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
In some places various wind slab layers are lying on surface hoar.
Performed ECTs and field observations have confirmed the existence of a weak snowack.

tendency.highlights: With the easing of the wind, the natural avalanche activity will gradually decrease.

avalancheActivity.highlights: In some localities up to 50 cm of snow has fallen. As a consequence of new snow and wind a high avalanche danger will prevail.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The new snow is lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack. As a consequence of new snow and a sometimes strong wind, large surface-area wind slabs formed in all aspects. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on wind-loaded slopes of all aspects above approximately 1800 m and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack.
Weak layers in the old snowpack are treacherous. In some places the avalanches can be triggered in deep layers of the snowpack and reach large size.

snowpackStructure.comment: New snow at low altitude. In some localities up to 50 cm of snow has fallen. In some regions up to 30 cm of snow has fallen above approximately 600 m. The new snow is lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. As a consequence of new snow and a sometimes strong northwesterly wind, avalanche prone wind slabs will form in all aspects. Field observations and weak layers in the old snowpack indicate poor snowpack stability.

avalancheActivity.comment:
The avalanche prone locations are widespread. As a consequence of new snow and wind, further wind slabs will form. Large to very large natural avalanches are possible over a wide area. In the regions exposed to heavier precipitation the avalanches can in isolated cases reach very large size and in some places endanger transportation routes situated at relatively high altitudes.
Without question, it is inadvisable to engage in backcountry touring.

snowpackStructure.comment: The new snow and wind slabs are bonding poorly with the old snowpack in all aspects. The wind slabs are prone to triggering. Over a wide area they will increase in size additionally. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

bulletins.highlights: The conditions are very dangerous for winter sport activities outside marked and open pistes. Remotely triggered and natural avalanches are to be expected.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Outside marked and open pistes a very dangerous avalanche situation will prevail. The danger exists in particular in alpine snow sports terrain.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Wind slabs are very prone to triggering in all aspects above the tree line, caution is to be exercised, including in areas close to the tree line. Avalanches can be released in near-surface layers of the snowpack.
Additionally avalanches can also penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size on west, north and east facing slopes. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and fresh avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.

As a consequence of the strong to storm force foehn wind from the north more natural avalanches are to be expected. This applies in case of releases originating from very steep leeward starting zones, in particular at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

On very steep slopes small and medium-sized loose snow avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
20 to 40 cm of snow has fallen since Thursday. On Friday the wind will be strong to storm force.
The wind will transport the new snow. Further wind slabs will form.

The snowpack will be prone to triggering over a wide area. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. Somewhat older wind slabs are lying on surface hoar. Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.

tendency.highlights: The off-piste conditions are very dangerous. Weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger.

bulletins.highlights: Backcountry touring and other off-piste activities call for great restraint.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The new snow and wind slabs represent the main danger. Outside marked and open pistes a very precarious avalanche situation will be encountered over a wide area.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Over a wide area over a wide area 25 to 40 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell on Thursday. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes above the tree line. The sometimes strong wind has transported the new snow. Above the tree line further wind slabs will form, especially at intermediate and high altitudes, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain at elevated altitudes.
On very steep slopes natural avalanches must be expected. Mostly the avalanches are medium-sized and can be released easily by a single winter sport participant. In some places the avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach large size. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger. The avalanche prone locations are prevalent and are barely recognisable, even to the trained eye. Backcountry touring calls for meticulous route selection.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area over a wide area 25 to 40 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen. In all aspects wind slabs formed.
The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line. The snowpack remains unstable over a wide area.

tendency.highlights: The snowpack remains unstable over a wide area. The snow sport conditions outside marked and open pistes are precarious. The avalanche danger will persist.

avalancheActivity.highlights: In the regions exposed to heavier precipitation the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent. The snowpack will be prone to triggering above approximately 1700 m.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can be released easily. New snow and wind slabs require caution. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released even easily by individual winter sport participants, especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line as well as in areas close to the tree line. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach very large size. The avalanche prone locations are numerous difficult to recognise.
Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger. A high (level 4) danger of natural avalanches exists.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The new snow and wind slabs are lying on surface hoar in particular on wind-protected shady slopes. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers very easily.
In addition the fresh wind slabs adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls are easily triggered. Large to very large natural avalanches are possible on wind-loaded slopes.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of the new snow the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of snowfall and the occasionally storm force northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last three days. This applies in particular in the regions exposed to the foehn wind and in areas close to the tree line, and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
The avalanche-prone wind slabs can be released easily, even by a single winter sport participant,.

On wind-loaded slopes and in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place medium-sized and large dry and moist avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some regions over a wide area 40 to 80 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Monday.
The sometimes storm force wind has transported a lot of snow.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and east facing slopes.

bulletins.highlights: Natural avalanches are to be expected.

avalancheActivity.highlights: A very dangerous avalanche situation will prevail. The danger exists in particular in alpine snow sports terrain.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The large quantity of fresh snow and the extensive wind slabs that are being formed by the strong westerly wind are very prone to triggering in all aspects above the tree line. Such avalanche prone locations are to be found also in areas close to the tree line.

As a consequence of new snow and wind natural avalanches are to be expected until late in the night. Avalanches can in some cases penetrate deep layers and reach large size. This applies on west, north and east facing slopes.

With the end of the snowfall, the natural avalanche activity will slowly decrease. In particular in the regions exposed to the foehn wind more natural avalanches are possible as a consequence of the strong to storm force northwesterly wind. Caution is to be exercised in particular in leeward starting zones.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some regions 10 to 20 cm of snow will fall until late morning. The wind will be moderate to strong. The wind will be storm force at times in particular in the Southern Zillertal Alps and High Tauern and along the border with South Tyrol. The wind will transport the new snow. Further wind slabs will form.

The snowpack will be prone to triggering over a wide area. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. Somewhat older wind slabs are lying on surface hoar. Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.

tendency.highlights:
Some snow will fall in some regions. The rain will give rise to softening of the snowpack at low and intermediate altitudes. As a consequence of a sometimes storm force wind from westerly directions, further wind slabs will form on Saturday.
The snow sport conditions outside marked and open pistes remain very dangerous. Avalanches can be released very easily. More natural avalanches are possible.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches are the main danger. Moist and wet avalanches are possible in the second half of the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of the strong northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days.
In particular intermediate and high altitudes as well as in places that are protected from the wind: Soft wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in areas close to the tree line. The wind slabs are clearly recognisable.
In particular steep sunny slopes and base of rock walls: Medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large dry and moist avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 15 to 20 cm of snow fell yesterday in all altitude zones. The strong wind has transported the fresh and old snow.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind. Shady slopes: The snowpack is dry and has a loosely bonded surface.
Sunny slopes: The solar radiation will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches are the main danger. Moist and wet avalanches are possible from the middle of the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Caution is to be exercised on very steep slopes, and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. Avalanches can be released, even by a single winter sport participant.
Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution. In some places the avalanches can be triggered in the old snow and reach quite a large size.
Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm sign.
As a consequence of the strong northerly foehn wind, fresh snow drift accumulations will form. High-altitude starting zones will be replenished as a consequence of the wind. Here small and medium-sized natural dry avalanches are possible.
In the typical avalanche paths the avalanches can as before reach fairly large size.
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation moist and wet avalanches are possible as the day progresses, even medium-sized ones.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 20 to 50 cm of snow has fallen since Sunday above approximately 1600 m. Until Saturday the wind will be strong.
Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind. The snowpack is unfavourably layered.
Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.
Some medium-sized to large natural dry avalanches have been released until Thursday.

tendency.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already increase in the late morning.

regions.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches are the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The sometimes strong wind has transported the new snow significantly. As a consequence of the strong northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days.
In particular intermediate and high altitudes as well as in places that are protected from the wind: Soft wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in areas close to the tree line. The wind slabs are clearly recognisable.
In particular steep sunny slopes and base of rock walls: Small and medium-sized dry and moist avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 15 to 20 cm of snow fell yesterday in all altitude zones. The strong wind has transported the fresh and old snow.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind. Sunny slopes: The solar radiation will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint. The wind slabs can be released easily or naturally in particular on steep shady slopes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Dry avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.
The fresh snow and in particular the sometimes large wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant.

snowpackStructure.comment: Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Fresh wind slabs are to be evaluated critically. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases by individual winter sport participants especially in little used terrain. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Mostly avalanches are medium-sized.

In addition the wind slabs in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain are prone to triggering in some cases still. Such avalanche prone locations are to be found on north, east and southeast facing slopes above the tree line.

Restraint is advisable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
10 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Thursday. In some regions 10 to 20 cm of snow will fall until late morning. The wind will be strong to storm force in some localities.
The sometimes storm force wind will transport the new snow. Sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs will form.

The snowpack will be in some cases prone to triggering. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.

tendency.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger.

bulletins.highlights: Dry snow slides and avalanches and slab avalanches are the main danger.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Especially adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls and on wind-loaded slopes small and medium-sized slab avalanches are possible as a consequence of new snow and wind. Single backcountry tourers can release avalanches in some places, including medium-sized ones.
avalancheActivity.comment: Avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found in particular on wind-loaded slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches are the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: Gullies and bowls where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially unfavourable. Avalanches can as before be released, in particular by large loads and reach medium size. Mostly small natural avalanches are possible below approximately 1800 m.

snowpackStructure.comment: The wind will be moderate to strong. In gullies and bowls above approximately 1800 m a lot of snow is lying. In the last few days sometimes deep wind slabs formed in particular in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. In some places wind slabs are lying on soft layers.
